children.name: chromium
auth.spec.js
Auth — Login Page Elements
TC01 - login page loads and URL contains auth/login
TC02-TC04 - login form fields and button are present and interactive
TC05 - forgot password link is visible
TC06 - forgot password link contains text Forgot
Auth — Valid Login
TC07 - valid credentials redirect to dashboard
TC08 - dashboard shows main navigation menu after login
TC09 - admin role sees Admin menu item on dashboard
TC10 - API bypass login reaches dashboard without UI form interaction
Auth — Invalid Login
TC11 - wrong password shows error alert
TC12 - wrong username shows error alert
TC13 - empty username field shows error
TC14 - empty password field shows error
TC15 - both fields empty shows error
TC16 - error message contains Invalid credentials text
TC17 - page stays on login URL after failed login
TC23 - invalid login shows error alert with correct message and stays on login page
Auth — Logout
TC18 - logout redirects to login page
TC19 - login form is visible after logout
Auth — Forgot Password
TC20 - forgot password link navigates to reset page
TC21 - reset page has a username input field
TC22 - clicking cancel on reset page navigates back to login
api.spec.js
API Mocking — Mock Success
TC01 - mocked employee list renders mocked names in table
TC02 - mocked response shows exactly 2 rows in the table
TC03 - mocked response shows second employee name in table
TC04 - page renders without error when mock returns valid payload
API Mocking — Mock Empty State
TC05 - mocking empty employee list shows No Records Found
TC06 - mocking empty directory shows zero result cards
TC07 - empty list mock returns zero clickable rows
API Mocking — Mock Errors
TC08 - mocking 500 error does not crash the browser
TC09 - mocking 404 does not crash the browser
TC10 - mocking 401 keeps user on the page or redirects
TC11 - after unrouting a mock the real data loads again
API Mocking — Slow Network
TC12 - slow mock delays the table from loading immediately
TC13 - after slow mock resolves table rows are visible
API Mocking — Intercept & Modify
TC14 - intercepted response shows injected name in table
TC15 - custom modify function changes a specific field in the response
dialogs.spec.js
Dialogs — Page Load
TC01 - job titles page loads successfully
TC02 - table has at least one row
TC03 - Add button is visible on job titles page
TC04 - delete icon is visible on each table row
Dialogs — Confirm Dialog
TC05-TC07 - confirm dialog has all required buttons with correct labels
TC08 - clicking No closes the dialog without deleting
TC09 - after clicking No the table row count is unchanged
TC10 - confirm dialog disappears after clicking No
Dialogs — Add Job Title Page
TC11 - clicking Add button navigates to Add Job Title page
TC12 - Add Job Title page has a heading
TC13 - heading text is Add Job Title
TC14 - Add Job Title page has a Save button
TC15 - Add Job Title page has a Cancel button
TC16 - clicking Cancel navigates back to job titles list
TC17 - submitting empty form shows required validation error
TC18 - add job title form has all required elements present
files.spec.js
Files — Page Load
TC01 - employee profile page loads successfully
TC02 - add attachment button is present on employee profile
TC03 - file input element exists after opening attachment dialog
Files — Upload
TC04 - uploading a valid PNG file shows success toast
TC05 - uploading a valid PDF file shows success toast
TC06 - uploaded file appears in the attachments table
TC07 - save button is visible after selecting a file
TC08 - file input accepts a file without throwing error
Files — Invalid Type
TC09 - uploading an invalid file type shows error or is handled
TC10 - file input is present after opening attachment dialog
TC11 - cancel button in attachment dialog closes without saving
Files — Download
TC12 - download event can be listened for on employee list page
TC13 - page does not throw errors when download is triggered
TC14 - employee profile page has expected URL structure
TC15 - attachments section is accessible by scrolling
forms.spec.js
Forms — Page Load
TC01 - add employee page loads successfully
TC02 - first name field is visible
TC03 - last name field is visible
TC04 - middle name field is visible
TC05 - save button is visible
TC06 - cancel button is visible
Forms — Single Field Input
TC07 - first name field accepts and retains text input
TC08 - last name field accepts and retains text input
TC09 - middle name field accepts and retains text input
TC10 - first name field can be cleared and refilled
TC11 - save with valid first and last name shows success toast
TC12 - cancel button navigates back to employee list
Forms — Validation Errors
TC13 - submitting empty form shows required error on first name
TC14 - validation error text says Required
TC15 - submitting only first name shows error on last name
TC16 - both empty fields produce at least two validation errors
TC17 - error input field has error styling class
TC18 - fixing required fields and resubmitting shows success
Forms — Dynamic Fields
TC19 - create login details toggle is present on add employee page
TC20 - login detail fields are hidden by default
TC21 - enabling the toggle reveals the username field
TC22 - enabling the toggle reveals the password field
TC23 - disabling the toggle hides the login fields again
notifications.spec.js
Notifications — Success Toast
TC01 - saving a new employee shows success toast
TC02 - success toast has oxd-toast--success CSS class
TC03-TC05 - success toast has correct title, message and close button
TC06 - success toast appears at bottom of page
TC07 - toast disappears automatically after a few seconds
Notifications — Dismiss Toast
TC08 - clicking close button dismisses the toast
TC09 - toast is not visible after manual dismiss
Notifications — Validation Errors
TC10 - submitting empty add employee form shows field error
TC11 - first name required error message is visible
TC12 - last name required error message is visible
TC13 - multiple validation errors appear at same time
TC14 - fixing all errors and saving shows success toast
TC15 - two separate saves produce two separate success toasts
TC16 - validation errors show correct text, styling and count simultaneously
search.spec.js
Search — Directory
TC01 - directory page loads successfully
TC02 - directory shows result cards on load
TC03 - search with known name returns results
TC04 - search with nonexistent name shows invalid message
TC05 - reset button restores all directory cards
TC06 - search input field is visible on directory page
TC07 - search button is visible on directory page
TC08 - reset button is visible on directory page
Search — Employee List Filter
TC09 - employee list has search input field
TC10 - searching by name reduces result count
TC11 - searching nonexistent name shows No Records Found
TC12 - reset clears filter and restores employees
TC13 - search field accepts typed input
Search — Autocomplete
TC14 - autocomplete input accepts text on employee list
TC15 - typing a prefix shows autocomplete suggestions
TC16 - selecting an autocomplete option fills the input field
tables.spec.js
Tables — Page Load
TC01 - employee list page loads successfully
TC02 - table has at least one row
TC03 - table shows rows by default
TC04-TC06 - employee list has all search controls visible and enabled
Tables — Sorting
TC07 - clicking a column header changes row order
TC08 - table rows are present after sorting
TC09 - clicking same header again reverses order
Tables — Pagination
TC10 - pagination control is present when multiple pages exist
TC11 - page 2 shows different records than page 1
TC12 - page 3 shows different records than page 2
TC13 - navigating back to page 1 restores first page records
TC14 - page 1 button has selected styling
Tables — Filtering
TC15 - filtering by a known name returns fewer rows
TC16 - filtering by nonexistent name shows no records message
TC17 - reset filter restores records
TC18 - edit button navigates to employee detail page
TC19 - employee table has correct structure with headers, rows and search input
